headline: Google urges EU top court to back ruling scrapping $1.7 billion antitrust fine
summary: Alphabet unit Google on Wednesday urged Europe's top court to dismiss EU antitrust regulators' appeal ‌against a lower court ruling that scrapped a €1.49 billion ($1.7 billion) fine, saying ‌the regulators' arguments were flawed.  The dispute reached the Court of Justice of the European Union ​after regulators appealed a 2024 General Court ruling that annulled the fine imposed on Google in 2019.  The lower court cited errors in the European Commission's assessment of the case.

content.title: Google urges EU top court to back ruling scrapping $1.7 billion antitrust fine
content.summary: Alphabet unit Google on Wednesday urged Europe's top court to dismiss EU antitrust regulators' appeal ‌against a lower court ruling that scrapped a €1.49 billion ($1.7 billion) fine, saying ‌the regulators' arguments were flawed.  The dispute reached the Court of Justice of the European Union ​after regulators appealed a 2024 General Court ruling that annulled the fine imposed on Google in 2019.  The lower court cited errors in the European Commission's assessment of the case.

headline: Oracle shares gain after report it leads race for Japanese government cloud contract (ORCL)
summary: Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) shares moved higher in premarket trading on Monday after a report suggested the company is leading the competition to provide cloud infrastructure services to the Japanese government. Oracle reportedly ahead in bidding processAccording to a Financial Times report citing seven people familiar with the discussions, Oracle is currently ahead of Amazon Web Services, Microsoft and Google in negotiations over the potential contract.

content.title: Oracle shares gain after report it leads race for Japanese government cloud contract (ORCL)
content.summary: Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) shares moved higher in premarket trading on Monday after a report suggested the company is leading the competition to provide cloud infrastructure services to the Japanese government. Oracle reportedly ahead in bidding processAccording to a Financial Times report citing seven people familiar with the discussions, Oracle is currently ahead of Amazon Web Services, Microsoft and Google in negotiations over the potential contract.

headline: Cypress Creek and Google Break Ground on America's Largest Solar Project
summary: Cypress Creek Energy and Google today joined community leaders, project partners, and elected officials to celebrate the start of construction on the first two phases of Steel River Energy Center, the nation's largest solar energy project to date. The groundbreaking event marked a major milestone for a project that will help meet growing electricity demand while showcasing Arkansas's role in strengthening America's energy and manufacturing supply chains.

content.title: Cypress Creek and Google Break Ground on America's Largest Solar Project
content.summary: Cypress Creek Energy and Google today joined community leaders, project partners, and elected officials to celebrate the start of construction on the first two phases of Steel River Energy Center, the nation's largest solar energy project to date. The groundbreaking event marked a major milestone for a project that will help meet growing electricity demand while showcasing Arkansas's role in strengthening America's energy and manufacturing supply chains.
